lessons.title: Supremacy of EU Law
lessons.description: Supremacy — sometimes called primacy — means that wherever EU law and national law conflict, EU law prevails. The national rule must be set aside, not just interpreted compatibly, but positively disapplied.

lessons.title: Direct Effect of Treaty Articles and Regulations
lessons.description: Direct effect is complementary to supremacy: where supremacy determines which law prevails, direct effect determines whether an individual can rely on EU law at all.

lessons.title: Direct Effect of Directives
lessons.description: Directives present a particular challenge because, under Article 288 TFEU, they are binding as to the result to be achieved but leave to national authorities the choice of form and methods of implementation.

lessons.title: Indirect Effect and Limits in Criminal Proceedings
lessons.description: Understanding indirect effect, and critically understanding its limits, is essential for FE-1 candidates.
